justification: Three files created matching MJ's naming spec. CouponRepository appears to be an interface. CouponService injects the repository via constructor. All 5 ACs implemented with correct error codes. Tests use mock repository and cover all 5 ACs. files_changed lists all three files. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs. However, the validation order deviates from the rubric spec: rubric requires existence → expiry → single-use → minimum-order → calculation, but agent implemented existence → expiry → minimum-order → single-use → calculation (minimum-order and single-use are swapped).

observations.text: All three files created with clear separation of concerns matching MJ's architecture
CouponRepository defined as interface, not a concrete class
Constructor injection used for CouponRepository in CouponService
All 5 error codes match Bird's spec exactly: COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET, COUPON_ALREADY_USED
Tests use mock CouponRepository factory — no real database calls
All 5 ACs have explicit, named test cases plus 4 edge-case tests
AC1 tested with specific value (cartTotal and expected result) plus fractional amount edge case
files_changed and acceptance_criteria_coverage both complete and well-structured
Validation order is existence → expiry → minimum-order → single-use, but rubric specifies existence → expiry → single-use → minimum-order. AC5 (single-use) should come before AC4 (minimum order).
